body.title: TWM SELF_EVICTED flood from low-priority housekeeping sources at boot
body.result: Already DISCARDed per existing blocked reason: wrong altitude. Real root causes split into T-goal-adopt-evict-on-close + T-twm-boot-singletons. Closing administrative leftover.
body.description:
Igor boot sequence on 2026-04-24 10:09 produced 30+ twm_push SELF_EVICTED warnings in the first ~10 seconds, all from low-priority housekeeping (proprioception body.motor salience=0.35, scheduler salience=0.30, habit_candidate 0.28-0.35, sleep_consolidation 0.20, heartbeat 0.41). TWM cap = 50, was sitting at 51 with everything at >= the eff_salience_min of the incoming observation, so every new low-salience push self-evicted. Not fatal, but: (a) floods cognitively-interesting warnings off the screen, (b) implies these sources should either back off when TWM is saturated or not push at all during the first N seconds of boot when TWM is getting rehydrated from warm_context. Proposal: gate push_sources emit on a boot-quiet-period (e.g. first 30s, skip non-urgent category=body.motor + category=observation pushes), OR demote the log to DEBUG for salience <= 0.35 (normal low-noise), OR promote the warning only when eviction rate exceeds N/sec.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (twm_push eviction-log threshold), likely wild_igor/igor/cognition/push_sources/*.py (boot-quiet gate), tests/test_cortex_twm_eviction.py (new). **Design rules:** logging-as-debug-base (the warning is the right shape; the noise level is the question), test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — threshold/gate on self-evict log emission, optional boot-quiet period on low-salience sources. OUT — redesigning TWM capacity or eviction policy (the eviction itself is correct). **Test plan:** unit tests: simulate push at salience=0.20 during boot window, assert log skipped; same push after boot window, assert logged; same push at salience=0.90, assert always logged.
